whyExists: When creating specialized classes (e.g. `Dog` and `Cat`), they share common attributes/methods (e.g. `name`, `eat()`). Inheritance lets a child class inherit all methods and attributes from a parent class, avoiding code duplication.
curiosityQuestion: What does `super().__init__()` do inside a child class constructor?
problemItSolves: Promotes code reuse by establishing hierarchy relationships between classes.
realWorldAnalogy: Biological inheritance: A child inherits eye color and DNA features from their parent, but also develops their own unique skills.
simpleExplanation:
Syntax:
`class ChildClass(ParentClass):`
- Child automatically gets all parent methods.
- Override methods by re-defining them in child.
- Call parent methods using `super()`.

examples.title: Basic Inheritance & Super

visualDiagram:
```mermaid
classDiagram
    Person <|-- Employee
    Person: name
    Employee: emp_id
```

stepByStepExecution.explanation: Calls Employee __init__, delegates name initialization to Person via super().

memoryDiagram.stack: Child instance contains parent + child attributes

namingRules: Use clean single inheritance hierarchies where possible.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call `super().__init__()` in child class `__init__`.
